policy.library_role: Library pages are concept-level wiki entries distilled from Articles, not a raw external-link shelf.
policy.external_links: External source links belong inside article/source references, not as the primary Library object.

items.title: Article to Library knowledge compression
items.description: A wiki entry for the editorial rule that Articles are judged notes, while Library pages compress repeated article concepts into reusable knowledge objects.

sections.heading: What this concept means
sections.body: Articles can remain close to an individual source, observation, or tool. Library entries should be more durable: they name the reusable concept, collect related Articles, and explain how the concept should be used later.

sections.heading: Use inside Knowledge Hub
sections.body: This page is not a public policy article. It is the working concept that keeps the site from becoming a link dump. When several Articles circle the same idea, the Library page becomes the stable home for that idea.

sections.heading: Update rule
sections.body: Add or revise a Library page when an Article no longer matters only as a standalone note. The Library page should summarize the pattern, list the related Articles, and expose the next useful reading or production path.

article_refs.title: The library layer should compress repeated curiosity into reusable entries
A useful wire is not a feed, it is a staging area
A tag is useful only when it changes retrieval

items.title: Production workflow automation
items.description: A concept page for small automation loops that clean, classify, summarize, compare, rename, extract, route, and publish creative work.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Automation is most useful when it disappears into repeated production tasks. The durable value is not a spectacular output, but a lower-friction loop around ingestion, classification, review, and publishing.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: Local scripts, AI-assisted cleanup, source routing, draft generation, metadata repair, publishing utilities, and repeated editorial operations belong on this shelf.

sections.heading: What does not belong here
sections.body: One-off tool announcements should stay in Wire or Article until they reveal a repeatable production behavior.

article_refs.title: The useful AI tool is the one that disappears into the workflow
Local AI belongs in the boring parts of the loop first
AIVideo Agent is part of the automation layer around moving images

items.title: Local AI infrastructure
items.description: A wiki page for treating local AI as infrastructure: cost control, privacy, latency, file access, offline work, and repeatability.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Local AI should not be judged only by whether it can replace a frontier model. Its infrastructure value is that it can run near files, folders, repos, and private constraints.

sections.heading: Operational value
sections.body: The most durable use cases are boring: classify notes, rewrite summaries, compare sources, rename assets, read project folders, and keep costs predictable.

sections.heading: Comparison axis
sections.body: Track local AI tools by privacy, cost, latency, context limits, file access, model quality, and whether they can become a daily loop.

article_refs.title: Local offline coding tools are infrastructure, not a novelty category
Local AI belongs in the boring parts of the loop first
Automatic1111 remains a useful reference for open image-generation workflows

items.title: Agentic coding and project memory
items.description: A concept page for AI coding systems that need stable project structure, repository reading, durable conventions, and tool-using agents.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: AI coding becomes useful when the project gives the agent stable memory surfaces: file conventions, source boundaries, skills, hooks, and repeatable review paths.

sections.heading: Repository reading
sections.body: Code search is not enough. Graph views, entry points, dependencies, ownership, and repeated concepts help both human and AI readers understand a repo faster.

sections.heading: Library use
sections.body: Group agent notes by operational behavior: coding assistant, repository map, project memory, local execution, review loop, and deployment support.

article_refs.title: Claude Code project structure is an editorial memory problem too
GitNexus suggests a useful shape for repository reading

items.title: Node graph memory and procedural systems
items.description: A concept page for keeping node-based work understandable through notes, graph context, reusable explanations, and related procedural examples.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Node graphs expose structure, but without memory they become archaeology. The Library should store why a graph exists, what behavior it produces, and which examples belong together.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: Houdini node inspection, Blender Geometry Nodes, Sverchok examples, reusable procedural setups, graph annotation ideas, and production memory around node systems.

sections.heading: How to update
sections.body: When an Article explains a node technique, link it here under the larger procedural family instead of leaving it isolated.

article_refs.title: Node-based work needs a memory layer outside the node graph
Sverchok and procedural Blender notes should stay grouped
Blender cloud animation notes belong with production memory

items.title: Spatial capture and reconstruction
items.description: A concept page for 3DGS, NeRF-like methods, multi-view reconstruction, capture constraints, review use, and production reliability.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Spatial capture is moving from impressive demos toward practical scene representation. The useful question is whether a method can support review, camera exploration, reconstruction, or web delivery.

sections.heading: Comparison axis
sections.body: Compare techniques by required views, cleanup steps, temporal handling, editability, rendering cost, camera freedom, and whether the result can enter a production pipeline.

sections.heading: Library grouping
sections.body: 3DGS, NeRF, multi-view reconstruction, radiance fields, and capture apps should live together so individual Articles can be compared as one production problem.

article_refs.title: Universal Beta Splatting extends 3DGS from reconstruction into motion
VGG-T and the pressure to make multi-view reconstruction usable

items.title: Rendering and look-development decisions
items.description: A concept page for rendering notes that are valuable because they connect tools, materials, preview, review, and final image decisions.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Rendering notes become knowledge when they explain what can be previewed, exchanged, trusted, automated, or carried into final output.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: Material interchange, viewport preview, physically based rendering, lightweight inspection, shader tests, and decisions between asset, material, renderer, review, and final output.

sections.heading: How to avoid link dumping
sections.body: A renderer or material tool should not be listed only by name. The Library entry should say what production decision it clarifies.

article_refs.title: Rendering notes become valuable when they connect tools to decisions
MaterialX and Filament rendering compatibility note

items.title: AI video as reference system
items.description: A concept page for using AI video as reference material: motion mood, impossible cameras, texture studies, lighting proposals, and image behavior.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: AI video does not need to be finished cinema to be useful. It can be a reference system for motion behavior, camera language, texture, lighting, transitions, and visual rhythm.

sections.heading: Production use
sections.body: Treat short clips as visual notes: extract what they reveal, then translate that into layout, animation, lighting, edit, shader, or design work.

sections.heading: Review rule
sections.body: Do not preserve AI video because it is novel. Preserve it when it names or demonstrates a reusable visual behavior.

article_refs.title: AI video is becoming a reference library before it becomes a cinema
AIVideo Agent is part of the automation layer around moving images

items.title: Visual reference and film language
items.description: A concept page for film, image, trailer, animation, and visual-culture notes that teach framing, texture, rhythm, tone, staging, or image behavior.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Visual reference is useful when it names a behavior: density, texture, distance, contrast, rhythm, surface, framing, time, or tone.

sections.heading: How to collect
sections.body: A film or image note should explain what it gives back to creative work. It should not stay at the level of admiration or trivia.

sections.heading: Use cases
sections.body: Shader tests, lighting passes, camera blocking, editing rhythm, motion study, art direction, interface mood, and production reference all belong here.

article_refs.title: Visual reference is a production asset, not decoration
Xenogenesis is a compact film-school source
Touch Me is a film-reference note about tone and form

recommendation_groups.title: Short film / early work
items: Xenogenesis — early James Cameron craft reference

recommendation_groups.title: Genre and tone
items: Touch Me — body-horror / science-fiction tone reference

recommendation_groups.title: Archive mood
items: Tarkovsky Polaroids — atmosphere, time, unresolved ordinary spaces

items.title: Book and reading map
items.description: A combined book recommendation and reading-map page. Individual book mentions should accumulate here by genre, theme, and use case.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Book references should not remain scattered as isolated links. This page collects them into a reusable reading map: what the book is useful for, which concept it supports, and what genre or theme it belongs to.

sections.heading: Grouping rule
sections.body: Group books by use: creative writing, film thinking, philosophy, history, archive design, AI/technology, and production thinking. Add individual source Articles only when they deepen one of those groups.

sections.heading: Update rule
sections.body: When a Wire or Article recommends a book, add it here under an existing group first. Create a new Library page only when the book becomes a recurring concept by itself.

article_refs.title: Books and films need the same treatment as tools
Bruno Schroeder's library note belongs with archive imagination

recommendation_groups.title: Archive / library imagination
items: Bruno Schroeder's library — physical archive as intellectual environment

recommendation_groups.title: Literature / essay reference
items: Dazai and Gomi notes — interpretation, guilt, literary reframing

recommendation_groups.title: Knowledge systems
items: NotebookLM / Obsidian workflow articles — reading as source-grounded compression

items.title: Film recommendation map
items.description: A combined film recommendation map. Film mentions should accumulate here by genre, visual problem, tone, and production lesson.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Film recommendations are more useful when they are grouped by what they teach: tone, staging, camera movement, texture, editing rhythm, performance intensity, or production constraint.

sections.heading: Grouping rule
sections.body: Keep genre groups, but attach each title to a creative use. A horror film, short film, animation reference, or trailer note should explain why it matters to image-making.

sections.heading: Update rule
sections.body: New film Articles should be added to this map before creating a standalone Library page. Create a standalone Library page only when a film becomes a recurring reference object.

article_refs.title: Touch Me is a film-reference note about tone and form
Xenogenesis is a compact film-school source
Film reference note on Possession

recommendation_groups.title: Science fiction / early craft
items: Xenogenesis — compact early-career production and visual habit source

recommendation_groups.title: Horror / body tone
items: Touch Me — tone, body horror, genre framing

recommendation_groups.title: Performance intensity / anti-remake logic
items: Possession — form tied to trauma, performance, and unstable tone

recommendation_groups.title: Animation / staging
items: Night on Bald Mountain — ominous staging and dense atmospheric animation

items.title: Archive and document AI
items.description: A concept page for OCR, document understanding, public archives, source capture, layout preservation, and long-term retrieval.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Archive work is not just storage. It is capture, layout, OCR, cleanup, metadata, search, retrieval, and later reuse.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: OCR tools, public archives, document AI benchmarks, layout extraction, library design, source maps, and durable research references.

sections.heading: Evaluation axis
sections.body: Judge archive tools by reliability, layout preservation, source traceability, search quality, and whether they help future writing.

article_refs.title: NDL OCR Lite fits the document-AI and archive workflow shelf
Bruno Schroeder's library note belongs with archive imagination
The archive should stay readable before it becomes large

items.title: Motion design and browser production tools
items.description: A concept page for lightweight animation, motion-design, browser tools, procedural graphics, and sketch-level production surfaces.

sections.heading: Core idea
sections.body: Not every useful tool has to be a full production suite. Lightweight browser or motion-design tools can be valuable for sketches, tests, quick editorial visuals, and motion studies.

sections.heading: What belongs here
sections.body: Pikimov, Cavalry-like procedural motion, lightweight video tools, design-to-motion utilities, and practical animation tests.

sections.heading: How to compare
sections.body: Compare by speed, export quality, timeline control, procedural ability, browser constraints, collaboration, and whether the result can feed a larger production workflow.

article_refs.title: Pikimov belongs on the lightweight motion-design shelf
AI video as reference material
